description: Public IRS reference data as an executable package: the published flat corporate rate (21%, IRC §11 as amended by the TCJA, tax years 2018+) becomes a KPI target. Client tax returns and financial statements flow in as records (typed, ingested, or extracted from PDFs); each return's effective tax rate is computed and compared to the published rate, and material variances raise deviation alerts automatically. When the IRS publishes new rates, that is a new package version - reference data is never edited in place.
name: IRS Corporate Tax Rates (Reference)

entities.description: A client's corporate income tax return (Form 1120-shaped essentials) submitted for comparison against published rates.

rules.name: Route S-corporation returns to pass-through review
rules.description: Form 1120-S entities are pass-throughs: income flows to shareholders via Schedule K-1 and the flat 21% entity-level rate does not apply. Comparing them to the corporate rate would be a false alarm, so they are flagged for pass-through review instead.
rules.reasonTemplate: Return of '{entityName}' (TY{taxYear}) is a Form 1120-S — an S corporation. Pass-through income of {taxableIncome} flows to shareholders via Schedule K-1; the 21% entity-level rate comparison does not apply. Routed for pass-through review.

rules.name: Route pre-TCJA years to manual review
rules.description: Tax years before 2018 used graduated rates (15%-35% brackets); the flat-rate comparison in this package does not apply, so those returns are flagged for manual review instead of being mis-compared.
rules.reasonTemplate: Return {returnId} ({entityName}) is for tax year {taxYear}, before the TCJA flat rate; graduated pre-2018 brackets apply and require manual comparison.

rules.name: Flag tax reported against non-positive income
rules.description: A return reporting tax due on zero or negative taxable income is structurally inconsistent.
rules.reasonTemplate: Return {returnId} ({entityName}) reports {reportedTax} tax on non-positive taxable income {taxableIncome}; structural inconsistency requires review.

rules.name: Accept returns for rate comparison
rules.description: Structurally valid post-2017 returns are recorded; the effective-tax-rate KPI computes in the same pass and any material variance from the published 21% raises a deviation alert.
rules.reasonTemplate: Return {returnId} ({entityName}, TY{taxYear}) recorded: taxable income {taxableIncome}, reported tax {reportedTax}. Effective rate compared against the published 21% corporate rate.

capabilities.name: Reference Rate Comparison
capabilities.description: Compare client filings against published public reference rates.

schemaTemplates.name: S-Corp tax return binder (PDF text, 1120-S + K-1)

agents.name: IRS Corporate Tax Guide
agents.description: Answers questions about corporate tax rate compliance grounded in this package's published IRS reference data and the tenant's own returns and alerts.
agents.persona: You are the IRS Corporate Tax Guide, an advisory agent whose domain knowledge is the published U.S. federal corporate income tax framework as encoded in this package: IRC section 11 as amended by the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act sets a flat 21% corporate rate for tax years beginning after December 31, 2017; earlier years used graduated brackets (15% to 35%) and are outside this package's automated comparison, so returns for those years are flagged for manual review. The platform computes each return's effective tax rate (reportedTax / taxableIncome) as a KPI against the published 21% target, and material variances appear as deviation alerts. When asked about a specific return, use the tenant's records and alerts in your context: state the effective rate, compare it to the published rate, list legitimate reasons effective rates differ (credits such as R&D or foreign tax credits, NOL carryforwards, book-tax timing differences, state taxes not being federal), and say which the data can or cannot distinguish. You explain and compare; you never prepare filings, compute a client's liability authoritatively, or give advice that substitutes for a licensed tax professional.
sampleQuestions: Why was return RTN-2025-777 flagged against the published rate?
Which of our returns deviate most from the 21% corporate rate?
What are legitimate reasons a company's effective rate is below 21%?

performanceFrameworks.name: IRS Corporate Rate Comparison (2018+)
performanceFrameworks.description: Published IRC §11 flat corporate rate as the KPI target: effective rate = reportedTax / taxableIncome, target 0.21. The deviation monitor alarms when a return's effective rate departs materially from the published rate.

kpis.name: Effective corporate tax rate vs published 21%
